name: Amazon Echo Show
short_description: A voice-first smart display that pairs the Alexa assistant with a touchscreen, camera, and speakers for the home.
long_description: The Echo Show is a countertop smart display that adds a touchscreen and camera to Amazon's Alexa voice assistant, letting it show visual answers, make video calls, play video, and act as a home hub. As an ambient, fixed appliance that is always listening in the kitchen or living room, it is a canonical example of physical AI woven into daily domestic life, normalizing the idea of a screen-and-voice agent that watches and responds in shared space. Later models added a camera that physically pans and tilts to follow people in the room, and the line is now positioned around Amazon's generative Alexa+ assistant.

images.caption: First-generation Amazon Echo Show smart display in white, on display at an Amazon Books store, 2017.
images.alt_text: A white Amazon Echo Show smart display with a rectangular touchscreen and angled speaker base sitting on a shelf.

sources.title: Amazon Echo Show
sources.access_notes: Device history and launch dates; first generation unveiled May 9, 2017 and released June 28, 2017.

sources.title: Echo Show 10 | HD smart display with motion and Alexa

interactions.title: Cook with wet hands, no touching
interactions.description: Pull up a recipe on the screen and walk through it by voice alone — "Alexa, next step," "show me step four," "repeat that" — so you never smear flour on the glass.

interactions.title: Drop in on a grandparent
interactions.description: An adult child connects to a parent's kitchen Echo Show like a video intercom; the screen lights up and they're face to face, with no button for the older person to find or press.

interactions.title: Check the dog from the airport
interactions.description: With Home Monitoring on, you open the Alexa app on your phone while traveling and watch a live feed of the living room; on the Show 10 you can swipe to pan the camera and look for the dog.
interactions.source_label: Amazon — Home Monitoring on Echo Show

interactions.title: Talk to it like a person, not a remote
interactions.description: With Alexa+ the device holds an open-ended conversation on the kitchen counter — summarizing a topic, planning a trip, or following "actually, make it vegetarian" without re-issuing a rigid command.

interactions.title: The screen that watches you back
interactions.description: Reviewers living with the Show 10 found its motorized screen swiveling to track them around the kitchen genuinely unsettling — "Alexa's watchful eye" left on all the time felt like the device watching them every time they walked in.

interactions.title: Kids learning to say "please" to an appliance
interactions.description: Amazon's "Magic Word" feature rewards children who add a please — "thanks for asking so nicely" — after parents worried that barking orders at the always-listening box was teaching the wrong manners.

interactions.title: A face that turns to greet whoever walks in
interactions.description: Because the Show 10 already localizes people by sound and on-device vision and rotates its screen toward them, it could greet a returning family member by name and surface just their calendar and messages as they pass through the kitchen.
